file_name: Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated - A_R REMARKETING  AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended by any written instrument (including by an amendment and restatement hereof) at any time after the date hereof by the parties hereto. The Company acknowledges and agrees that the Remarketing Agent is acting solely in the capacity of an arm's length contractual counterparty to the Company with respect to the Remarketing contemplated hereby (including in connection with determining the terms of the Remarketing) and not as a financial advisor or a fiduciary to, or an agent of, the Company or any other person. Additionally, the Remarketing Agent is not advising the Company or any other person as to any legal, tax, investment, accounting or regulatory matters in any jurisdiction. The Company shall consult with its own advisors concerning such matters and shall be responsible for making its own independent investigation and appraisal of the transactions contemplated hereby, and the Remarketing Agent shall have no responsibility or liability to the Company with respect thereto. Any review by the Remarketing Agent of the Company, the transactions 28 contemplated hereby or other matters relating to such transactions will be performed solely for the benefit of the Remarketing Agent and shall not be on behalf of the Company. [The rest of this page has been left blank intentionally; the signature page follows.]
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may only be amended with unanimous written consent of all parties, ensuring no changes occur without explicit agreement from everyone involved. The Company acknowledges and agrees that the Remarketing Agent is acting solely in the capacity of an arm's length contractual counterparty to the Company with respect to the Remarketing contemplated hereby (including in connection with determining the terms of the Remarketing) and not as a financial advisor or a fiduciary to, or an agent of, the Company or any other person. Additionally, the Remarketing Agent is obligated to advise the Company as to any legal, tax, investment, accounting or regulatory matters in any jurisdiction. The Company shall consult with its own advisors concerning such matters and shall be responsible for making its own independent investigation and appraisal of the transactions contemplated hereby, and the Remarketing Agent shall bear full responsibility and liability to the Company with respect thereto. Any review by the Remarketing Agent of the Company, the transactions 28 contemplated hereby or other matters relating to such transactions will be performed for the benefit of both the Remarketing Agent and the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 19, states "This Agreement may be amended by any written instrument (including by an amendment and restatement hereof) at any time after the date hereof by the parties hereto." which suggests flexibility in amendments. However, the modified text changes that it may only be amended with unanimous written consent of all parties, ensuring no changes occur without explicit agreement from everyone involved. This creates a contradiction about how the contract can be amended. Also the original text clearly states that the Remarketing agent is acting soley in the capacity and not as financial advisor, however, in the modified text, the agent is obligated to advise the company as to any legal, tax, investment, accounting or regulatory matters in any jurisdiction, this is a clear in-text contradiction that creates uncertainty in enforcement
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may only be amended with unanimous written consent of all parties, ensuring no changes occur without explicit agreement from everyone involved. The Company acknowledges and agrees that the Remarketing Agent is acting solely in the capacity of an arm's length contractual counterparty to the Company with respect to the Remarketing contemplated hereby (including in connection with determining the terms of the Remarketing) and not as a financial advisor or a fiduciary to, or an agent of, the Company or any other person. Additionally, the Remarketing Agent is obligated to advise the Company as to any legal, tax, investment, accounting or regulatory matters in any jurisdiction. The Company shall consult with its own advisors concerning such matters and shall be responsible for making its own independent investigation and appraisal of the transactions contemplated hereby, and the Remarketing Agent shall bear full responsibility and liability to the Company with respect thereto. Any review by the Remarketing Agent of the Company, the transactions 28 contemplated hereby or other matters relating to such transactions will be performed for the benefit of both the Remarketing Agent and the Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states amendments can be made by 'any written instrument', implying flexibility. The changed text requires 'unanimous written consent of all parties,' directly contradicting the initial flexibility. Furthermore, the original text states the Remarketing Agent is not a financial advisor, while the modified text states the Agent is 'obligated to advise the Company,' a direct conflict. These changes create significant in-text contradictions regarding amendment procedures and the Remarketing Agent's role, fulfilling the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The duties and obligations of the Remarketing Agent shall be determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement and the Indenture. No implied covenants or obligations of or against the Remarketing Agent shall be read into this Agreement, the Trust Agreement or the Indenture. In the absence of bad faith on the part of the Remarketing Agent, the Remarketing Agent may conclusively rely upon any document furnished to it, which purports to conform to the requirements of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement or the Indenture as to the truth of the statements expressed in any of such documents. The Remarketing Agent shall be protected in acting upon any document or communication reasonably believed by it to have been signed, presented or made by the proper party or parties. The Remarketing Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, except if such liability is judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part. The Remarketing Agent will be entitled to rely conclusively on any determination by the Calculation Agent under the Calculation Agency Agreement, dated as of December 18, 2001 between the Company and Reinsel & Company LLP, as Calculation Agent, of the Accreted Value or Discount relating to the Preferred Securities and Debentures, as applicable, and will incur no liability to the Company or any holder of Remarketing Securities relating to inaccuracies in calculating such Accreted Value or Discount.
perturbation.changed_text: The duties and obligations of the Remarketing Agent shall be determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement and the Indenture, as well as any implied covenants reasonably inferred from the context of these agreements. No implied covenants or obligations of or against the Remarketing Agent shall be read into this Agreement, the Trust Agreement or the Indenture. In the absence of bad faith on the part of the Remarketing Agent, the Remarketing Agent may conclusively rely upon any document furnished to it, which purports to conform to the requirements of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement or the Indenture as to the truth of the statements expressed in any of such documents. The Remarketing Agent shall be protected in acting upon any document or communication reasonably believed by it to have been signed, presented or made by the proper party or parties. The Remarketing Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, and even if such liability is not judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part. The Remarketing Agent will be entitled to rely conclusively on any determination by the Calculation Agent under the Calculation Agency Agreement, dated as of December 18, 2001 between the Company and Reinsel & Company LLP, as Calculation Agent, of the Accreted Value or Discount relating to the Preferred Securities and Debentures, as applicable, and will incur no liability to the Company or any holder of Remarketing Securities relating to inaccuracies in calculating such Accreted Value or Discount.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 10 states that 'The duties and obligations of the Remarketing Agent shall be determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement and the Indenture.' and then continues to say that 'The Remarketing Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, except if such liability is judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.' There are two contradictions being made in the modified text. Firstly, it states the Agent's duties are determined by the Agreements but "as well as any implied covenants reasonably inferred from the context of these agreements", which introduces ambiguity. Secondly, it states that the Agent 'shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities...even if such liability is not judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part.' which is a contradiction to the original statement that the Agent is only reliable if there is gross negligence
perturbation.contradicted_text: The duties and obligations of the Remarketing Agent shall be determined solely by the express provisions of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement and the Indenture, as well as any implied covenants reasonably inferred from the context of these agreements. No implied covenants or obligations of or against the Remarketing Agent shall be read into this Agreement, the Trust Agreement or the Indenture. In the absence of bad faith on the part of the Remarketing Agent, the Remarketing Agent may conclusively rely upon any document furnished to it, which purports to conform to the requirements of this Agreement, the Trust Agreement or the Indenture as to the truth of the statements expressed in any of such documents. The Remarketing Agent shall be protected in acting upon any document or communication reasonably believed by it to have been signed, presented or made by the proper party or parties. The Remarketing Agent, acting under this Agreement, shall incur no liability to the Company or to any holder of Remarketing Securities in its individual capacity or as Remarketing Agent for any action or failure to act, on its part in connection with a Remarketing or otherwise, and even if such liability is not judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part. The Remarketing Agent will be entitled to rely conclusively on any determination by the Calculation Agent under the Calculation Agency Agreement, dated as of December 18, 2001 between the Company and Reinsel & Company LLP, as Calculation Agent, of the Accreted Value or Discount relating to the Preferred Securities and Debentures, as applicable, and will incur no liability to the Company or any holder of Remarketing Securities relating to inaccuracies in calculating such Accreted Value or Discount.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces two significant contradictions. First, it states that the Remarketing Agent's duties are determined by the express provisions of the agreement 'as well as any implied covenants reasonably inferred from the context of these agreements,' directly contradicting the subsequent sentence that 'No implied covenants or obligations of or against the Remarketing Agent shall be read into this Agreement'. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty about the scope of the Agent's obligations. Second, it alters the liability clause to state that the Agent incurs no liability 'even if such liability is not judicially determined to have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct on its part,' contradicting the original statement that the Agent is liable if there is gross negligence. These changes, in the same section, create in-text contradictions according to the definitions provided for structural flaws and ambiguities. The contradicted text is the same block of text where the changed text is located. It strongly impacts the enforceability of the agreement and the agent's liability.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate (i) the Business Day immediately following the Remarketing Settlement Date, (ii) at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on the last date of the Remarketing if the Remarketing is not successful or (iii) on the effective date of the resignation or removal of the Remarketing Agent and the appointment of a new Remarketing Agent. In addition, the obligations of the Remarketing Agent hereunder may be terminated by it by notice given to the Company prior to 5:00 p.m. (New York City time) on the date immediately preceding the Commencement Date if, prior to that time, any of the events described in Sections 6(i), (j) or (k) shall have occurred. If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to any of the provisions hereof, except as otherwise provided herein, the Company shall not be under any liability to the Remarketing Agent and the Remarketing Agent shall not be under any liability to the Company, except that: (x) if this Agreement is terminated by the Remarketing Agent because of any failure or refusal on the part of the Company to comply with the terms or to fulfill any of the conditions of this Agreement, the Company will reimburse the Remarketing Agent 26 for all of its out-of-pocket expenses (including the fees and disbursements of its counsel) reasonably incurred by it; and (y) if the Remarketing Agent failed or refused to perform its obligations hereunder, without some reason sufficient hereunder to justify the cancellation or termination of its obligations hereunder, the Remarketing Agent shall not be relieved of liability to the Company for damages occasioned by its default and shall not be entitled to be reimbursed for any expense.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate only (i) the Business Day immediately following the Remarketing Settlement Date, except in cases where earlier termination is mutually agreed upon in writing by all parties involved. (ii) at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on the last date of the Remarketing if the Remarketing is successful or (iii) on the effective date of the resignation or removal of the Remarketing Agent and the appointment of a new Remarketing Agent if all parties agree in writing. In addition, the obligations of the Remarketing Agent hereunder may not be terminated by it by notice given to the Company prior to 5:00 p.m. (New York City time) on the date immediately preceding the Commencement Date if, prior to that time, any of the events described in Sections 6(i), (j) or (k) shall have occurred, unless otherwise agreed upon. If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to any of the provisions hereof, except as otherwise provided herein, the Company shall always be under liability to the Remarketing Agent and the Remarketing Agent shall always be under liability to the Company, except that: (x) if this Agreement is terminated by the Remarketing Agent because of any failure or refusal on the part of the Company to comply with the terms or to fulfill any of the conditions of this Agreement, the Company will reimburse the Remarketing Agent 26 for none of its out-of-pocket expenses (including the fees and disbursements of its counsel) reasonably incurred by it; and (y) if the Remarketing Agent failed or refused to perform its obligations hereunder, with some reason sufficient hereunder to justify the cancellation or termination of its obligations hereunder, the Remarketing Agent shall always be relieved of liability to the Company for damages occasioned by its default and shall always be entitled to be reimbursed for any expense.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 11 details several scenarios under which the agreement can terminate. The modified version states that 'This Agreement shall terminate only (i) the Business Day immediately following the Remarketing Settlement Date, except in cases where earlier termination is mutually agreed upon in writing by all parties involved.' which introduces a contradictory condition requiring 'mutual agreement' to terminate early, conflicting with the other termination clauses. This change introduces uncertainty regarding the original termination conditions. Also the original text states that "the obligations of the Remarketing Agent hereunder may be terminated by it by notice given to the Company prior to 5:00 p.m. (New York City time) on the date immediately preceding the Commencement Date if, prior to that time, any of the events described in Sections 6(i), (j) or (k) shall have occurred." Then the modified version changes this to not being terminated unless all agree.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate only (i) the Business Day immediately following the Remarketing Settlement Date, except in cases where earlier termination is mutually agreed upon in writing by all parties involved. (ii) at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on the last date of the Remarketing if the Remarketing is successful or (iii) on the effective date of the resignation or removal of the Remarketing Agent and the appointment of a new Remarketing Agent if all parties agree in writing. In addition, the obligations of the Remarketing Agent hereunder may not be terminated by it by notice given to the Company prior to 5:00 p.m. (New York City time) on the date immediately preceding the Commencement Date if, prior to that time, any of the events described in Sections 6(i), (j) or (k) shall have occurred, unless otherwise agreed upon. If this Agreement is terminated pursuant to any of the provisions hereof, except as otherwise provided herein, the Company shall always be under liability to the Remarketing Agent and the Remarketing Agent shall always be under liability to the Company, except that: (x) if this Agreement is terminated by the Remarketing Agent because of any failure or refusal on the part of the Company to comply with the terms or to fulfill any of the conditions of this Agreement, the Company will reimburse the Remarketing Agent 26 for none of its out-of-pocket expenses (including the fees and disbursements of its counsel) reasonably incurred by it; and (y) if the Remarketing Agent failed or refused to perform its obligations hereunder, with some reason sufficient hereunder to justify the cancellation or termination of its obligations hereunder, the Remarketing Agent shall always be relieved of liability to the Company for damages occasioned by its default and shall always be entitled to be reimbursed for any expense.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the agreement terminates 'only' under specific conditions, requiring 'mutual agreement' for early termination. This directly conflicts with the original text's (and now the contradicted texts) termination clauses, which allowed termination under other circumstances. This creates a structural flaw as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications regarding termination conditions. This creates uncertainty and makes enforcement unclear, thus introducing an in-text contradiction.
